reasoning: The State of Emergency (SOE) for Samoa was declared on March 31, 2025, and explicitly set to expire on April 29, 2025. It was primarily enacted due to a severe power crisis caused by generator failures. Temporary generators arrived on April 5, and as of April 20, 2025, the Electricity Power Corporation of Samoa officially canceled the regular power rationing program on Upolu, stating that generation capacity now meets demand. Because the immediate crisis triggering the SOE has effectively been resolved (as evidenced by the return to normal electricity distribution), the government has no functional reason to extend the state of emergency beyond its scheduled April 29 conclusion, nor is there any evidence of an alternate emergency declaration leading up to the April 21 knowledge cutoff. There remains a small risk that the SOE is nominally extended for administrative reasons (e.g. to maintain tax exemptions on energy imports until permanent generators arrive in August), but standard practice in returning to regular power distribution strongly suggests it will be allowed to lapse before April 30.
